summary:
Renal glycosuria explains why someone can have normally filtered but excessively excreted sugars via urination when not truly diabetic; alternatively, interference could falsely trigger Benedict’s reagent reaction under non-diabetic circumstances.
```

The summary focuses only on these two potential causes—renally mediated issues versus testing artifacts—and does so concisely based solely upon provided evidence regarding abnormal urinary findings juxtaposed against apparently normal serum values measured accurately using standard techniques. ```markdown
